flows.name: DispatchSingleQueuedMessage
flows.trigger: Async job picks up ConversationMessage with Status=Queued — green-ai design (DD_CM_02 continuation). Layer 0: triggered inline by ConversationController:SendConversationReply.

steps.action: SELECT ConversationMessage WHERE Id=@MessageId and verify Status=Queued
steps.source: ConversationRepository.cs:GetConversationMessage:62 — SQL: SELECT TOP(1) * FROM dbo.ConversationMessages WHERE Id = @Id
steps.layer0_vs_greenai: Layer 0 skips this check — passes message data directly. Green-ai: guard read required (INV_CD_01).

steps.action: SELECT Conversation + phone numbers + gateway provider + CustomerId for dispatch context
steps.source: ConversationRepository.cs:GetConversation:121 — SQL: SELECT c.*, cpn.PhoneCode SenderPhoneCode, cpn.PhoneNumber SenderPhoneNumber, cpn.GatewayProvider SenderGatewayProvider, cpn.CustomerId FROM Conversations c INNER JOIN ConversationPhoneNumbers cpn ON cpn.Id = c.ConversationPhoneNumberId WHERE c.Id = @id

steps.action: Call IOutboundMessageSender.SendMessage(profileId, senderPhoneCode, senderPhoneNumber, senderGatewayProvider, partnerPhoneCode, partnerPhoneNumber, partnerName, text) → returns SmsLogId (long)
On SUCCESS: UPDATE ConversationMessages SET SmsLogId=@smsLogId, Status=Sent (2) WHERE Id=@MessageId
steps.source: ConversationRepository.cs:UpdateConversationMessageStatus:79 — SQL: if exists (...WHERE SmsLogId=@smsLogId) update SET Status=@status WHERE SmsLogId=@smsLogId
steps.transformation_note: Layer 0 updates by SmsLogId (post-send, smsLogId known). Green-ai: update by Id (pre-send, Id known from INSERT-FIRST) — requires new SQL in green-ai.

steps.action: On FAILURE (MessageNotSentException): UPDATE ConversationMessages SET Status=Failed (4) WHERE Id=@MessageId

steps.source: green-ai design — transformation_required (no Layer 0 return value — CreateAndSendConversationMessage returns long smsLogId)

flows.invariant: INV_CD_01: ConversationMessage MUST exist with Status=Queued BEFORE any gateway call (step 1 guard — green-ai design; Layer 0 has no equivalent guard).
flows.partial_state_risk: LOW — row exists (Queued) before gateway call. On failure: Queued→Failed. On success: Queued→Sent. No orphan state possible.

flows.name: DeliveryStatusUpdateFlow
flows.trigger: DLR (Delivery Receipt) event from SMS gateway arrives as StatusUpdatesEvent with batch of StatusUpdate(SmsLogId, StatusCode)

steps.action: Map each StatusUpdate.StatusCode → ConversationMessageStatus: SmsReceived1→Delivered(1), SmsSendNotReceived2/Unknown136→Pending(0), GatewaySendError6/GatewayUnableToDeliver7/MessageDeleted9/Rejected146/Undeliverable156→Failed(2), default→Pending(0)
Batch UPDATE ConversationMessages SET Status=@Status WHERE SmsLogId IN @SmsLogIds (grouped by status value)
steps.source: ConversationRepository.cs:UpdateConversationMessageStatuses:84 — SQL: update ConversationMessages set Status=@Status where SmsLogId in @SmsLogIds

flows.transformation_note: Layer 0 updates by SmsLogId. Green-ai: must also update by SmsLogId for DLR (SmsLogId is the gateway correlation key). Status values must be remapped: Layer 0 Delivered=1/Failed=2 → green-ai Delivered=3/Failed=4.

unknowns.reason: Layer 0 has no retry for conversation dispatch (fire-and-forget). Green-ai retry policy is a design decision.
No background job infrastructure in Layer 0 for this flow. Green-ai: polling interval vs event-driven not decided.
